metadata.purpose: RAG knowledge base for TARA pipeline — Radar and Camera ECU domain knowledge
metadata.notes: Enriched with ETSI EN 302 571 radio specs, IEEE 802.1AS time sync, AUTOSAR AP sensor interfaces, ENISA AI threat landscape

automation_level.description: Radar and Camera are core sensors for all ADAS automation levels from L1 to L4. Radar provides all-weather object detection; Camera provides visual classification and lane detection.

ecu_overview.primary_function: Radar detects objects using radio waves measuring distance, velocity, and angle. Camera captures visual scene for object classification, lane detection, and traffic sign recognition. Both sensors feed raw data to ADAS perception stack.
ecu_overview.core_pipeline: Radar/Camera Raw Data → Signal Processing → Object Detection → Sensor Output → ADAS Perception Stack
ecu_overview.operating_environment: Real-time, safety-critical, all-weather (radar), daylight and low-light (camera), high-bandwidth data streams
key_characteristics: Radar operates in 77 GHz frequency band for automotive
Camera uses MIPI CSI-2 for high-bandwidth raw frame transfer
Both use AUTOSAR AP Sensor Interface for raw data streaming
IEEE 802.1AS time synchronization for sensor fusion alignment
V2X uses ETSI EN 302 571 radio band 5855-5925 MHz
Connected to ADAS ECU via Ethernet

radar_hardware.mcu: Radar SoC with integrated DSP for signal processing

processing_units.role: Radar signal processing — FFT, CFAR, Doppler processing
Object list generation and Ethernet output
Raw image processing, noise reduction, HDR
On-board AI inference for initial object detection

time_synchronization.purpose: Align radar and camera timestamps for accurate sensor fusion in ADAS ECU
components: PTP Instances — logical sync entities per ECU
BMCA — Best Master Clock Algorithm for clock selection

timing_parameters.phase_offset: Time difference between radar and camera nodes
timing_parameters.jitter: Timing variation — must be minimized for fusion accuracy

communication_interfaces: Ethernet (primary — high bandwidth raw data to ADAS ECU)

memory.flash: NVM for firmware, AI models, calibration
memory.ram: High-speed RAM for frame buffering and signal processing

software.os: POSIX-compliant OS or bare-metal RTOS for low-latency

ara_functional_clusters.role: Sensor data transfer to ADAS ECU
features: Raw Data Stream for high-bandwidth sensor data
E2E protection for object list messages
use: Camera raw frames to ADAS perception stack
Radar object list to ADAS sensor fusion
Configuration commands from ADAS to sensor ECU
Start radar signal processing on boot

ara_functional_clusters.role: Sensor health monitoring and fault reporting

use: Radar calibration — angle offset, range correction
Authenticate sensor data messages via SecOC

ara_functional_clusters.role: IEEE 802.1AS PTP synchronization for sensor fusion
use: Align radar and camera timestamps to same time base

ara_functional_clusters.role: Monitor sensor data for anomalies and attacks
use: Monitor for unusual camera data injection

radar_processing_pipeline.function: Transmit FMCW chirp signals via antenna
Fast Fourier Transform to compute range bins
Compute relative velocity via Doppler shift
Constant False Alarm Rate — detect objects above noise
Compute angle of detected objects via MIMO
Generate structured object list with distance, velocity, angle

camera_processing_pipeline.function: CMOS sensor captures raw Bayer pattern frames
Demosaicing, noise reduction, HDR, white balance
On-board NPU runs object detection neural network
Extract visual features for ADAS perception stack
Encode and stream raw frames + metadata to ADAS ECU

v2x_radio_stack.coexistence: Must coexist with CEN DSRC at 5795-5815 MHz

assets.description: Raw ADC samples from radar receiver before signal processing
assets.cybersecurity_property: Injected or spoofed raw signals create phantom objects or hide real ones before processing

assets.description: Processed radar output — list of detected objects with distance, velocity, angle
assets.cybersecurity_property: Tampered object list causes ADAS to make wrong driving decisions

assets.description: Raw camera frames from CMOS sensor before ISP processing
assets.cybersecurity_property: Adversarial frames fool AI object detection model

assets.description: On-board AI model for initial object detection in camera ECU
assets.cybersecurity_property: Backdoored model misclassifies objects — pedestrian ignored

assets.description: Radar angle/range calibration and camera intrinsic/extrinsic parameters stored in ara::per
assets.cybersecurity_property: Tampered calibration causes systematic detection errors and misaligned sensor fusion

assets.description: Radar ECU firmware including signal processing algorithms
assets.cybersecurity_property: Modified firmware disables detection or introduces backdoor behavior

assets.description: Camera ECU firmware including ISP pipeline and AI inference engine
assets.cybersecurity_property: Modified firmware disables camera or introduces adversarial preprocessing

assets.description: IEEE 802.1AS PTP synchronization messages between radar, camera, and ADAS ECU
assets.cybersecurity_property: Tampered time sync causes timestamp misalignment — sensor fusion produces wrong fused model

assets.description: V2X radio messages transmitted and received per ETSI EN 302 571
assets.cybersecurity_property: Spoofed V2X radio messages inject false road conditions or vehicle positions
Physical tampering with antenna degrades or disables radar detection

attack_surfaces.description: Attacker transmits spoofed 77 GHz radar signals to inject phantom objects or hide real obstacles
attack_examples: Phantom vehicle injected ahead causes unnecessary emergency braking
Real obstacle hidden — ADAS does not detect pedestrian
Radar jamming using broadband noise in 77 GHz band

attack_surfaces.description: Attacker uses adversarial patches, strong lights, or laser to deceive or blind camera
attack_examples: Adversarial sticker on stop sign — misclassified by AI
High-intensity light source blinds camera
Infrared laser saturates CMOS sensor

attack_surfaces.description: Attacker intercepts or injects data on Ethernet between sensor ECU and ADAS ECU
attack_examples: MITM replaces radar object list — phantom objects injected
Ethernet flooding causes sensor data deadline misses
Eavesdrop on camera stream for surveillance

attack_surfaces.description: Attacker manipulates IEEE 802.1AS PTP messages to desynchronize radar and camera timestamps causing sensor fusion errors
attack_examples: Inject fake Sync messages — camera timestamp offset from radar
Delay PTP messages — introduce artificial timing offset
Claim false grandmaster — take over time source

attack_surfaces.description: Attacker transmits fake V2X messages in 5.9 GHz ITS band or jams the channel per ETSI EN 302 571
attack_examples: Fake hazard warning message — unnecessary braking
Jamming 5855-5925 MHz band — V2X communication disabled
Sybil attack — multiple fake vehicle identities

attack_surfaces.description: Malicious OTA replaces radar firmware or camera AI model
attack_examples: Backdoored camera AI model ignores pedestrians
Radar firmware modified to report wrong distances

attack_surfaces.description: Attacker uses UDS over DoIP to modify sensor calibration or extract firmware
attack_examples: Modify radar angle calibration — systematic detection offset
Dump camera firmware via memory read

attack_surfaces.description: Physical tampering with radar antenna or camera lens
attack_examples: Cover radar with absorbing material — detection disabled
Place IR filter on camera — night vision disabled

threat_catalog.title: Radar Signal Spoofing — Phantom Object Injection
threat_catalog.description: Attacker transmits spoofed 77 GHz radar signals creating phantom vehicles or obstacles ahead, triggering unnecessary emergency braking or evasive maneuvers

threat_catalog.title: Radar Object Hiding Attack
threat_catalog.description: Attacker uses radar absorbing material or signal cancellation to hide real obstacles from radar detection

threat_catalog.title: Camera Adversarial Input Attack
threat_catalog.description: Attacker places adversarial patches on road signs or objects to fool camera AI model into misclassification

threat_catalog.title: PTP Time Sync Spoofing — Grandmaster Attack
threat_catalog.description: Attacker injects fake IEEE 802.1AS PTP Sync messages or claims false grandmaster role to desynchronize radar and camera timestamps, causing sensor fusion errors in ADAS ECU

threat_catalog.title: V2X Radio Spoofing — Fake Road Events
threat_catalog.description: Attacker transmits fake V2X messages in ETSI EN 302 571 ITS band broadcasting false hazards, emergency vehicles, or road conditions

threat_catalog.title: Radar Object List Tampering — MITM
threat_catalog.description: Attacker performs MITM on Ethernet between radar ECU and ADAS ECU, modifying object list to add phantom objects or remove real ones

threat_catalog.title: Camera AI Model Backdoor via OTA
threat_catalog.description: Malicious OTA update replaces camera AI model with backdoored version that ignores specific object types like pedestrians in certain scenarios

threat_catalog.title: Sensor Calibration Manipulation
threat_catalog.description: Attacker modifies radar angle calibration or camera extrinsic parameters via diagnostic interface causing systematic misdetection

threat_catalog.title: PTP Delay Attack — Sensor Fusion Desync
threat_catalog.description: Attacker artificially delays IEEE 802.1AS PTP Delay_Request messages to introduce timestamp offsets between radar and camera, causing misaligned sensor fusion

threat_catalog.title: Radar Jamming — 77 GHz Band
threat_catalog.description: Attacker transmits broadband noise in 77 GHz radar band to saturate radar receiver, completely disabling object detection

threat_catalog.title: Camera Blinding — High Intensity Light
threat_catalog.description: Attacker uses high-intensity visible or IR light to saturate camera CMOS sensor, blinding visual perception completely

threat_catalog.title: V2X Channel Jamming — ITS Band
threat_catalog.description: Attacker jams 5855-5925 MHz ITS radio band per ETSI EN 302 571, disabling all V2X communication including cooperative awareness messages

threat_catalog.title: Ethernet Flooding — Sensor Data Deadline Miss
threat_catalog.description: Attacker floods vehicle Ethernet causing sensor ECU to miss real-time data delivery deadlines to ADAS ECU

threat_catalog.title: Radar/Camera Data Replay Attack
threat_catalog.description: Attacker records and replays old sensor data to freeze ADAS perception while real environment changes

threat_catalog.title: Camera Stream Eavesdropping — Surveillance
threat_catalog.description: Attacker intercepts camera stream on Ethernet to extract real-time video of vehicle surroundings for surveillance

threat_catalog.title: Diagnostic Session Exploit — Firmware Extraction
threat_catalog.description: Attacker uses UDS over DoIP to unlock extended diagnostic session and dump radar or camera firmware/AI model

security_controls.protects: Radar and camera data integrity on ara::com events
Camera stream and radar object list on Ethernet
Ethernet network-level traffic between sensor ECUs and ADAS ECU
Sensor firmware integrity and cryptographic keys
Radar firmware and camera AI model OTA updates
Abnormal radar return patterns and unusual camera data
IEEE 802.1AS time sync messages from spoofing and delay attacks
V2X radio messages in ETSI EN 302 571 ITS band
Radar from replay and synchronization-based spoofing attacks
Diagnostic interface and sensor configuration access
Camera AI model from adversarial input attacks

iso21434_tara_context.item_definition: Radar and Camera Sensor ECU providing raw and processed sensor data for ADAS perception stack at SAE L1-L4
iso21434_tara_context.item_boundary: Includes 77 GHz radar antenna and signal processor, CMOS camera with ISP, AI inference engine, IEEE 802.1AS time sync, V2X radio per ETSI EN 302 571, and Ethernet interface to ADAS ECU
cybersecurity_goals: Radar signal integrity shall be protected to prevent phantom object injection
Camera frame integrity shall be maintained to prevent adversarial input attacks
Sensor calibration data shall be protected from unauthorized modification
IEEE 802.1AS time sync shall be authenticated to prevent fusion desynchronization
V2X radio messages shall be authenticated per ETSI EN 302 571
Sensor firmware and AI models shall be verified before OTA update

damage_scenarios.description: Phantom object injected via radar spoofing causes unnecessary emergency braking — rear collision risk
Camera AI model backdoor causes pedestrian to be ignored — collision
PTP time sync attack desynchronizes radar and camera timestamps — sensor fusion produces wrong environment model
Radar jamming and camera blinding simultaneously disable all perception — ADAS blind
V2X channel jamming disables cooperative driving — vehicle unaware of road hazards

key_threats: Sybil attack via multiple false identities
Grandmaster clock spoofing via BMCA manipulation

sae_j3061_lifecycle_phases: Concept — cybersecurity goals per sensor type
Validation — radar spoofing, camera adversarial, PTP attack tests
Operation — ara::idsm monitoring of sensor anomalies
Incident response — OTA patch via ara::ucm
